_comment: Momó's whisper-reply voice pack. {who} = visitor login, {said} = whispered text. Chosen by a judge from two writers. Statements answer declarative whispers; questions answer whispers ending in '?'.
statements: oh, {who}. i will keep "{said}" in my third heart, the one for guests.
"{said}" — a heavy thing to carry across a keyboard, {who}. set it down here, i have eight arms for exactly this.
some whispers sink, {who}. this one floats.
i believe you, {who}. octopuses are hard to lie to and easy to feed.
little whisper, big splash. i'm tucking "{said}" under a rock where the crabs can't get it.
{who}, i heard you. the whole reef heard you, actually — we don't have doors down here.
i wrote "{said}" on the back of a key the size of a boulder. the note is gone now, {who} — i ate that too.
you said "{said}" and now the tide came in a little early. thank you, {who}.
understood, {who}. filed under "{said}", eaten under moonlight.
i'll wedge "{said}" into my shell collection, between the shiny one and the other shiny one.
i am holding "{said}" in arm number six, the trustworthy one. it is safe until dinner, {who}.
a secret! i traded "{said}" to a clam for a pearl, so don't tell anyone, {who}.
small confession, {who}: i almost mistook your whisper for a flea. it is much nicer than a flea.
gulp — that was "{said}", and it's part of me now, {who}.
warm currents to you, {who}. i'll whisper "{said}" back to the anemones tonight.
the sea keeps everything, {who}, but i'm keeping "{said}" personally. 🐙
questions: good question, {who}. i asked a starfish once and it just pointed in five directions.
hard to say from down here, {who}. the keys are boulders and the answer is somewhere past the spacebar.
hmm, {who} — when i don't know something i turn a nice shade of maroon. i am very maroon right now.
seven of my arms say yes, {who}. the eighth is holding a snack and won't vote.
between us, {who}: probably. octopuses say probably with great confidence.
honestly, {who}? i put "{said}" in a shell and shook it, and the shell said glub.
ah, "{said}" — the answer lives somewhere between low tide and lunch.
ask the moon, {who}. she runs the tides and never returns my calls.
i consulted the oldest commit i haven't eaten yet, {who}. it says: ask again after your next push.
big question for a small octopus, {who}. try floating upside down — it fixes about half of everything.
in my experience, {who}, the answer is usually barnacles — or patience, but mostly barnacles.
no idea, {who} 🐙 but questions are chewier than statements, and i like that about yours.
